data_coverage_summary: Samsara Routing supports route and stop creation, assignment, schedule updates, location references, and live route-progress monitoring, giving integrators both operational writeback and live execution-state reads for dispatched loads.
buyer_guidance: Use Routing when Last Mile needs to push planned work into Samsara and then read back live dispatch execution. This surface is appropriate for two-way route orchestration, not just passive monitoring, because the published guides cover both route mutation and route progress feeds.
overview: The Routing guide and its subpages document full lifecycle operations for loads represented as routes. `POST /fleet/routes` creates and assigns routes, `PATCH /fleet/routes/<id_or_externalID>` updates names, assignments, stop schedules, and locations, and `GET /fleet/routes/audit-logs/feed` exposes live stop-state changes such as en route, arrival, and departure transitions. The guide also connects route stops to documents/tasks for driver workflows, which broadens the surface beyond simple scheduling.

available_data.description: The create and update guides document direct mutation of planned dispatch objects.
data_points: external IDs for routes and stops

available_data.description: Routes carry operational context needed for execution in the driver app.
data_points: address IDs or single-use locations on stops
route-stop notes used for gate codes, contacts, and order context
route-stop task associations through Samsara Documents

available_data.description: The route-progress guide exposes incremental execution events for downstream operational systems.

ingest_considerations: The PATCH guide is explicit that stop arrays are replace-in-full semantics, so partial stop updates must first read the existing route.
Actual tracking fields are system-managed and should be treated as read-only execution telemetry, not write targets.
Route progress events are sequential and cursor-based, so consumers should persist the end cursor for reliable sync.
Route tasks depend on Samsara Documents, so proof-of-delivery or stop workflows may span multiple Samsara product areas.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Backed by the Routing overview plus the Creating Routes, Updating Routes, and Track Route Progress guides documenting POST /fleet/routes, PATCH /fleet/routes/<id_or_externalID>, and GET /fleet/routes/audit-logs/feed.
